evidence: `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` still exports deprecated alias `reportRuntimeError` (line 56) and deprecated type alias `RuntimeErrorOptions` (line 62), keeping old and new error-entry paths alive.

`src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts` remains an active compatibility facade with runtime warning logic (lines 22-33) and a public deprecated accessor (line 47), rather than a narrowed migration boundary.

`src/types/generationAndShots.ts` is a flat cross-domain type barrel (lines 4-11) and `src/shared/lib/toolConstants.ts` centralizes unrelated tool IDs/routes (lines 9-28), both acting as broad dependency hubs.

Assigned media-gallery files show coordination split across multiple modules (`useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts`, `useMobileInteractions.ts`, `useMediaGalleryItemState.ts`, `ShotActions.tsx`, `LightboxShell.tsx`) with callback/state threading instead of a tighter seam.

`CreativePartnerPane.tsx` mixes marketing copy rendering, tooltip interaction state machine, iframe embed wiring, and navigation concerns in a single page component (notably lines 91-149 and 171-186).

`ShotActions.tsx` owns UI rendering plus non-trivial behavioral branching for navigation/add flows (lines 106-141), while related state/control concerns are split into separate hooks/files (`useMediaGalleryItemState.ts`, `useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts`).

`LightboxShell.tsx` carries a very wide prop surface across modal behavior, edit modes, responsive flags, tasks-pane layout, refs, and accessibility strings (props defined lines 25-56), indicating orchestration leakage at component boundary.
Multiple wrappers/barrels (`tooltip.tsx`, `mobile/index.ts`, `generationAndShots.ts`) improve import ergonomics but blur ownership boundaries when combined with already-large coordinator components.

The lightbox/edit stack repeatedly re-projects the same state into multiple shapes (`imageEditValue`, `inpaintingState`, `transformState`, `generationState`) in `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts`, then rethreads those fields again in consumers.

Both image and video lightbox render flows build near-duplicate large `workflowBar` and `workflowControls` memo objects with very long dependency arrays (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx`).

Compatibility wrappers add extra local indirection (`run*Operation` plus `*LegacyAdapter`) in `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts`, while mutation inputs also carry non-operative compatibility fields in `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts`.

Failure contracts vary by module: `safePlay` returns `Promise<boolean>` on error (`src/shared/lib/media/safePlay.ts:3-14`), `useTaskStatusCounts` returns partial data with `degraded/failedQueries` (`src/shared/hooks/useTaskStatusCounts.ts:36-43,147-154`), `generateVideo` returns `{ success, error? }` (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:103-107,608-730`), and `fetchToolSettingsSupabase` primarily throws `ToolSettingsError` but also returns fallback defaults for some failures (`src/shared/lib/toolSettingsService.ts:291-372`).

`edgeRequest` exports two concurrent body-parse contracts: `OperationResult` (`parseJsonBodyStrict`) and legacy `{ body?, errorResponse? }` (`parseJsonBodyStrictResponse`), increasing handler-level branching (`supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts:195-223`).

`generationTaskRepository` exposes both status-preserving mapping APIs and status-dropping ID-only APIs (`getPrimaryTaskMappingsForGenerations` vs `getPrimaryTaskIdsForGenerations`), while consumers like task cache logic depend on status for degraded behavior (`src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts:108-185`, `src/shared/lib/generationTaskCache.ts:50-64`).

Root-task filtering contract is duplicated in separate API surfaces instead of centralized: `useTaskStatusCounts` hardcodes JSON-path null checks (`src/shared/hooks/useTaskStatusCounts.ts:16-34`) while repository helpers delegate to shared orchestration filtering (`src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts:92-94`).

`generateVideoService` keeps an overloaded request-builder input that mixes V2 grouped params with backward-compat scalar fields (`TravelRequestBodyInput`), then normalizes defaults silently before casting to `TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput` (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:432-487,723`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts:344-352`).

`src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts` is marked deprecated with `@removalTarget 2026-06-30` but still exports a live accessor and only logs after target (`warnIfPastRemovalTarget`) instead of enforcing migration.
`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` keeps deprecated aliases (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`) with the same removal target, preserving old call patterns as first-class API.
`src/shared/hooks/useContentResponsive.ts` is a deprecated alias hook that still forwards behavior directly, so old imports remain valid indefinitely.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` still accepts broad legacy field shapes (`starting_video_path`, `video_edit_mode`, `source_video_*`) and can infer legacy mode paths, extending mixed-shape support rather than shrinking it.
`src/shared/lib/taskConfig.ts` states DB is source of truth but retains a large hardcoded task-type registry fallback, keeping dual config paths active.

holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles shows `src/shared/hooks/` has 85 production files and `src/shared/lib/` has 73 production files, indicating flat-directory overload well beyond the >10 mixed-concern threshold.

Assigned files `src/shared/hooks/useAutoSaveSettings.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useCopyToClipboard.ts`, `src/shared/lib/ProjectScopedCache.ts`, `src/shared/lib/Slot.tsx`, and `src/shared/lib/caseConversion.ts` are one-line re-export shims to deeper packages (`hooks/settings`, `hooks/clipboard`, `lib/cache`, `components/ui`, `lib/utils`).

Low-fan-in stragglers remain at top-level paths: imports of `@/shared/lib/Slot` are effectively a single coverage test reference; `@/shared/lib/ProjectScopedCache` and `@/shared/lib/caseConversion` each have ~2 import sites.

`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/` imports the legacy root hook paths directly (for example `../useCopyToClipboard`, `../useContentResponsive`), reinforcing old placement instead of testing canonical package entrypoints.

findings.summary: Deprecated error/supabase facades are still first-class dependency hubs across runtime code.
evidence: `handleError.ts` keeps deprecated compatibility exports (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`) live, so old call sites continue to compile unchanged.

`legacySupabaseFacade.ts` still exposes deprecated `getLegacySupabaseClient()` with lifecycle policy metadata and runtime warning, indicating the shim is still in active path rather than isolated migration edge.
Assigned gallery hooks import and depend on the central error facade directly, reinforcing hub coupling from feature code into a compatibility-heavy shared entrypoint.
findings.suggestion: Enforce a shrinking adapter boundary: keep compatibility exports only in migration-only entry modules, migrate feature call sites to canonical APIs (`handleError` only, runtime supabase client only), then block new imports via lint rule/path ban and remove legacy exports when import count reaches zero.

findings.summary: Global barrels/constants centralize unrelated concerns and increase cross-module coupling.
evidence: `generationAndShots.ts` aggregates multiple type sources into a canonical barrel, encouraging broad imports from many features instead of narrower domain contracts.
`toolConstants.ts` mixes tool IDs, routes, and URL construction in one shared file used by page and shared components.
`CreativePartnerPane.tsx` imports `TOOL_ROUTES` directly, creating dependency from page UI to global shared routing constants instead of a feature-local navigation contract.
findings.suggestion: Split shared hubs by bounded context: domain type entrypoints per feature area and tool-specific route modules; expose small, purpose-scoped contracts (e.g., `homeNavigationContract`, `galleryTaskContracts`) instead of one global barrel for each concern.

findings.summary: Media gallery behavior is decomposed into broad prop/state bags rather than cohesive ownership slices.
evidence: `useMediaGalleryHandlers` requires a large argument object spanning navigation, modal, filter, and lightbox state setters (lines 16-29), indicating orchestration spread across modules.
`ShotActions.tsx` accepts four grouped bags (`selector`, `status`, `quickCreate`, `actions`) and still contains branching interaction logic, showing boundary complexity moved but not reduced.
`useMediaGalleryItemState.ts` duplicates UI state ownership (info open, selector open, create-shot modal) that must stay in sync with external handlers.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a feature-level controller/view-model for gallery item interactions that owns shot actions, tooltip/modal state, and mobile tap flow behind a small command API; keep presentational components mostly stateless.

findings.summary: CreativePartnerPane mixes static content, interaction policy, and navigation responsibilities.
evidence: The pane contains dense static narrative markup and also implements tooltip open/disable state transitions with mobile-specific branching (lines 93-137).
It coordinates navigation and external link CTA behavior in the same component (lines 171-186), coupling informational content with app-routing behavior.
Tooltip/mobile utility dependencies are pulled directly into the content pane, making ownership harder to reason about for future changes.
findings.suggestion: Split into (1) static content sections and (2) a dedicated `EcosystemTooltipController` or hook for open/disable/touch policy; pass only minimal callbacks into the content component so copy/layout edits do not touch interaction logic.

findings.summary: Inline edit state is projected into multiple overlapping shapes, increasing drift risk.
evidence: `useInlineEditState` constructs a very large `imageEditValue` object, then separately returns `inpaintingState`, `transformState`, and `generationState` with many of the same sources/functions.
Consumers such as `InlineEditView` and lightbox panels remap these same handlers/fields again, creating multiple manual mapping layers for one feature surface.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical edit view-model contract and expose narrow selectors (for panel/canvas/actions) derived from that contract, instead of rebuilding parallel object graphs in each layer.

findings.summary: Image and video lightboxes duplicate large workflow model construction logic.
evidence: Both files build `workflowBar` and `workflowControls` via long `useMemo` blocks with mostly shared keys (`onAddToShot`, `onCreateShot`, `showTick...`, optimistic handlers, navigation handlers, etc.).
Both maintain large dependency arrays for these duplicated objects, making behavior updates and bug fixes require dual edits with high miss risk.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared workflow-model factory (or hook) that accepts mode flags (`isVideo`, `isSpecialEditMode`, `isInpaintMode`) and shared handlers, then reuse it in both render paths.

findings.summary: Compatibility adapters add reversible wrappers that obscure core behavior.
evidence: `useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` defines `runAddToShotOperation`/`runAddToShotWithoutPositionOperation`/`runCreateShotOperation` returning `OperationResult`, then immediately re-wraps each with legacy adapters that throw via `toOperationResultError`.

`useGenerationMutations` mutation inputs include optional compatibility fields not used by the DB operation (`projectId?` in update location; `shotId?` only for side-channel event emission in star toggle), widening call contracts beyond true mutation requirements.
findings.suggestion: Choose one boundary contract per operation path (typed result or thrown error) and isolate legacy bridging at module edges; also split operational mutation inputs from optional UI metadata to keep core function signatures honest.

findings.summary: Annotation button behavior is implemented twice with divergent UI details.
evidence: Both `InlineEditView` and `LightboxLayout` implement their own `AnnotationButtons` logic: selected-shape lookup, free-form toggle, delete action, and positioning from `getDeleteButtonPosition()`.
The two implementations already diverge in styling tokens/placement (`absolute` vs `fixed`, different color sets), which makes annotation behavior/theming changes easy to apply inconsistently.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared annotation-controls component with style variants (inline vs lightbox) and keep shape-selection/toggle/delete behavior in one place.

findings.summary: Shared APIs encode recoverable failures with incompatible result shapes.
evidence: `safePlay` returns `false` on failure (`safePlay.ts:11-14`) instead of typed error context.
`useTaskStatusCounts` returns partial success with `degraded` and `failedQueries` (`useTaskStatusCounts.ts:147-154`).
`fetchToolSettingsSupabase` throws `ToolSettingsError` for most failures but returns defaults in specific error-string paths (`toolSettingsService.ts:353-371`).
`generateVideo` reports failures via `{ success: false, error }` (`generateVideoService.ts:142-145,725-729`).
findings.suggestion: Adopt one shared recoverable-failure contract (for example a discriminated `Result<T, E>` with `ok`, `code`, `recoverable`, `message`) and migrate these APIs to it; keep adapters only at UI boundaries.

findings.summary: Edge request parsing exposes two parallel public interfaces for the same operation.
evidence: `parseJsonBodyStrict` returns `OperationResult<Record<string, unknown>>` (`edgeRequest.ts:195-205`).
`parseJsonBodyStrictResponse` returns legacy `{ body?, errorResponse? }` envelope (`edgeRequest.ts:211-223`).
The module already standardizes response envelopes via `edgeErrorResponse`, so mixed parse return shapes create unnecessary handler adaptation paths (`edgeRequest.ts:95-117`, `rateLimit.ts:190-200`).
findings.suggestion: Deprecate and remove `parseJsonBodyStrictResponse`; standardize handlers on `OperationResult` and provide a single helper to convert failures into `Response` at call sites.

findings.summary: Repository exposes ID-only helper that discards mapping status needed by consumers.
evidence: `getPrimaryTaskMappingsForGenerations` returns status-rich `GenerationTaskMapping` (`generationTaskRepository.ts:108-171`).
`getPrimaryTaskIdsForGenerations` strips status and returns only `Map<string, string | null>` (`generationTaskRepository.ts:173-185`).
Cache preload logic relies on status (`query_failed`, `missing_generation`) for degraded telemetry and behavior (`generationTaskCache.ts:52-64`).
findings.suggestion: Make status-preserving mappings the only public repository contract; move any ID-only projection to internal call sites so failure semantics are not dropped at API boundaries.

findings.summary: Parent-task filtering logic is duplicated across APIs with separate path lists.
evidence: `useTaskStatusCounts` hardcodes `PARENT_TASK_REFERENCE_PATHS` and applies repeated `.is(path, null)` (`useTaskStatusCounts.ts:16-34`).
`generationTaskRepository` exposes `applyParentTaskFilter` delegating to shared `applyRootTaskFilter` (`generationTaskRepository.ts:92-94`).
Billing/orchestrator reference utilities maintain their own extraction/filter surface (`billing.ts:49-120`), indicating multiple API entry points for the same contract.
findings.suggestion: Centralize orchestrator/parent-task reference filtering into one exported contract used by hooks, repositories, and edge helpers to avoid behavior drift in sibling queries.

findings.summary: Travel request builder accepts mixed legacy/new param bags with silent defaulting.
evidence: `TravelRequestBodyInput` includes V2 grouped fields plus many backward-compat scalar fields (`generateVideoService.ts:432-452`).
`normalizeTravelRequestBodyInput` silently fills defaults (`seed: 0`, `generationMode: 'timeline'`, basic phase config) when fields are omitted (`generateVideoService.ts:454-487`).
Final call casts built body to `TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput` (`generateVideoService.ts:723`) instead of constructing directly from canonical typed input in `types.ts`.
findings.suggestion: Split request-building into explicit constructors (`fromV2Params`, `fromLegacyParams`) and keep a strict canonical output type end-to-end without `as unknown as` casting.

findings.summary: Deprecated alias modules remain operational APIs without hard migration cutover
evidence: `legacySupabaseFacade.ts` documents removal by `2026-06-30` but `getLegacySupabaseClient()` still returns the runtime client and only emits `console.error` after the date.
`handleError.ts` exports deprecated alias symbols (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`) with removal target metadata, but no runtime/build-time enforcement to stop new/continued usage.
`useContentResponsive.ts` is a pure deprecated forwarding alias, so migration progress depends entirely on voluntary import cleanup.
findings.suggestion: Add enforceable migration gates: block deprecated imports in CI, convert deprecated exports to thin wrappers that throw in non-test environments after target date, and run codemods to replace imports with canonical APIs (`getSupabaseClient`, `handleError`, `useViewportResponsive`).

findings.summary: Join-clips compat layer still normalizes multiple legacy payload shapes
evidence: `JoinClipsTaskCompatParams` includes both canonical fields and many legacy fields (`starting_video_path`, `ending_video_path`, `video_edit_mode`, `source_video_*`, `portions_to_regenerate`).
`toCanonicalJoinClipsTaskInput()` still infers mode from legacy path shapes (`clipSource.kind === 'legacy_paths' ? 'legacy' : 'multi_clip'`), preserving legacy semantics inside current flow.
`legacyStructureVideo.ts` still exports deprecated travel structure field contracts, indicating parallel old/new payload surfaces are still part of the active type layer.
findings.suggestion: Split compat parsing into a dedicated ingestion boundary and make internal APIs accept only canonical task input. Add an explicit one-way legacy translator module, then remove legacy fields from public task creation params once callers are migrated.

findings.summary: Task type config migration is incomplete due to dual hardcoded and DB registries
evidence: `taskConfig.ts` declares DB `task_types` as source of truth but keeps a large `HARDCODED_TASK_TYPE_CONFIG` fallback with visibility/category values.
`taskTypeUtils.ts` independently hardcodes task-type sets (`JOIN_CLIPS_TYPES`, `TRAVEL_TYPES`, `CHARACTER_ANIMATE_TYPES`), creating another parallel registry.
When task taxonomy changes, both modules can drift independently, which is migration debt from static config to runtime-backed config.
findings.suggestion: Centralize task taxonomy in one canonical provider (DB cache + typed fallback snapshot generated from the same source), and have `taskTypeUtils` derive predicates from that provider instead of local sets.

findings.summary: Join-clips adapter internals are overloaded with legacy and canonical orchestration logic
evidence: `joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` combines validation, mode boundary checks, clip-source resolution, legacy rejection policy, video-edit resolution, field stripping, telemetry callback shaping, and task creation handoff in one file.
The params interface is a large mixed bag with many optional fields, which increases defensive branching and weakens intention-revealing control flow.
This complexity is local implementation debt rather than pure boundary architecture because most branches are compatibility mechanics within one function chain.
findings.suggestion: Refactor into small explicit steps with typed discriminated unions: `parseLegacyInput`, `parseCanonicalInput`, `validateExclusiveModes`, `buildCanonicalInput`, and keep task creation call in a thin wrapper. This reduces branch density and clarifies invariants.

findings.summary: Preloader uses filename substring sentinel instead of explicit media metadata contract
evidence: `preloader.ts` skips URLs containing `'_joined_frame.jpg'` to avoid invalid thumbnails; behavior is encoded as a magic string check inside core preload flow.
This couples preload logic to one naming convention and hides decision intent from callers, creating brittle local branching.
`mediaTypeHelpers.ts` already normalizes media shapes/IDs, but no explicit preload eligibility contract is used, forcing ad-hoc filtering in `preloader.ts`.
findings.suggestion: Move skip logic to an explicit predicate contract (e.g., `isPreloadableMedia` or metadata flag) and pass that decision into preloader, removing hardcoded filename pattern checks from core queue logic.

findings.summary: Low-fan-in compatibility shims remain in `shared/lib` root and obscure actual package boundaries.
evidence: Each file is a one-line re-export to a more specific location: `lib/cache/ProjectScopedCache`, `components/ui/Slot`, and `lib/utils/caseConversion`.

Importer sampling shows these root aliases are now sparse (`Slot` only covered by `moduleCoverageConsolidated.test.ts`; `ProjectScopedCache` and `caseConversion` each have ~2 call sites), matching straggler behavior.
`src/shared/lib/` is already a 73-file mixed directory in holistic metrics; keeping leftover aliases there increases navigational noise.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) create `src/shared/lib/compat/` and move these three alias files there as explicit legacy entrypoints; 2) update importers to canonical targets (`@/shared/lib/cache/ProjectScopedCache`, `@/shared/components/ui/Slot`, `@/shared/lib/utils/caseConversion`) and keep temporary compat re-exports only where external callers still require them; 3) remove compat files once import count reaches zero. Validation commands: `rg -n "@/shared/lib/(ProjectScopedCache|Slot|caseConversion)" src`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, and run targeted tests for touched modules.

findings.summary: Root-level hook aliases keep `shared/hooks` as a catch-all instead of enforcing subpackage ownership.
evidence: `useAutoSaveSettings.ts` re-exports from `shared/hooks/settings/useAutoSaveSettings`, and `useCopyToClipboard.ts` re-exports from `shared/hooks/clipboard/useCopyToClipboard`.
`useContentResponsive.ts` is a deprecated alias to `shared/hooks/useViewportResponsive`, but callers and tests still consume the old root path.
holistic metrics show `src/shared/hooks/` has 85 files, indicating flat overload; retaining compatibility aliases at the root keeps the overload alive instead of converging on feature folders.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) define canonical hook entrypoints by domain (`shared/hooks/settings`, `shared/hooks/clipboard`, `shared/hooks/responsive`); 2) migrate internal imports in order: production hooks/components first, then tests, then remove root aliases or keep them in `shared/hooks/legacy/` with expiry comments; 3) add lint rule or import restriction to block new root-level alias imports. Validation commands: `rg -n "@/shared/hooks/use(AutoSaveSettings|CopyToClipboard|ContentResponsive)" src`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, and run the affected hook tests.

findings.summary: Compatibility aliases are distributed across core folders without a single lifecycle boundary, weakening ownership clarity.
evidence: `useContentResponsive` is marked deprecated while other aliases in the same pattern are not, so migration intent is inconsistent at the package level.
Alias files bridge unrelated layers (for example `shared/lib/Slot.tsx` re-exporting a UI component), so top-level package names no longer map cleanly to responsibilities.
No dedicated compatibility namespace is used in assigned files, making historical paths and canonical ownership indistinguishable during navigation.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) introduce explicit compatibility namespaces (`shared/hooks/legacy`, `shared/lib/compat`) and move all alias surfaces there; 2) standardize alias metadata (deprecation owner/remove-by) and keep canonical implementations only in domain folders; 3) remove or gate legacy imports via CI checks after migration. Validation commands: `rg -n "export \{ .* \} from '@/shared" src/shared/hooks src/shared/lib`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, and targeted test runs for touched consumers.

findings.summary: Deprecated Supabase compatibility facade remains a high-fan-in cross-module dependency hub.
evidence: `legacySupabaseFacade.ts` is marked deprecated with removal target 2026-06-30, but structure metrics show `src/integrations/supabase/legacy/` avg_fan_in 67.0 and large importer spread across shared hooks/components and tool modules.
Coupling matrix includes strong edge `src/shared/hooks/ -> src/integrations/supabase/legacy/` (36), so legacy path is still used as a live architectural seam rather than a narrow shim.
README positions this as a compatibility layer governed by import-budget checks, yet usage concentration indicates migration has not reduced architectural dependency surface.
findings.suggestion: Move from passive budget tracking to staged boundary reduction: phase 1 block new imports with lint/no-restricted-imports for `@/integrations/supabase/legacy/*`; phase 2 migrate highest-volume importer directories first (`src/shared/hooks/`, then `src/shared/hooks/shots/`, then tool hooks) to runtime client APIs; phase 3 keep legacy facade as thin adapter with importer count <=5 before removal date and fail CI when exceeded.

findings.summary: Core runtime contract modules conflate operational API and governance/deprecation metadata, blurring ownership boundaries.
evidence: `handleError.ts` exports runtime behavior (`handleError`, `handleAndRethrow`) together with governance constants and deprecated aliases, despite being presented as the runtime facade.
`legacySupabaseFacade.ts` exports policy metadata and runs runtime warning logic in the same transitional API module.
README’s governance sections imply explicit contracts, but contract-bearing runtime files remain multi-purpose, so module role clarity (runtime vs migration governance) is inconsistent.
findings.suggestion: Split ownership by extracting governance/deprecation metadata into dedicated policy modules and keeping runtime facades behavior-only. Leave deprecated aliases in explicit `compat/` entrypoints that re-export canonical runtime APIs, and document those `compat/` modules as the only migration surfaces.

findings.summary: Generation/shot contracts are centralized in a broad root `src/types/` package despite existing domain type directories, creating structural ambiguity.
evidence: `src/types/generationAndShots.ts` is the canonical barrel and re-exports root-level generation/shot files, reinforcing root package centralization.

Holistic structure shows `src/types/` has 12 mixed-concern files and high avg_fan_in (22.1), while `src/domains/generation/types/` also exists, indicating duplicate organizational centers for one domain.
Broad importer spread into `src/types/` from many directories suggests package placement is not aligned to bounded domain ownership.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) create/confirm target folder `src/domains/generation/types/` as the sole generation/shot contract package and add a local `index.ts` barrel; 2) move `generationParams.ts`, `generationMetadata.ts`, `generationRow.ts`, and `shot.ts` from `src/types/` into that domain folder; 3) keep `src/types/generationAndShots.ts` temporarily as a compatibility barrel re-exporting from `src/domains/generation/types`; 4) update imports in descending impact order (`src/shared/hooks/`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/**`, `src/shared/components/**`); 5) validate with `rg -n "@/types/(generationAndShots|generationParams|generationMetadata|generationRow|shot)" src`, then `npm run typecheck`, then `npm run quality:check`; 6) remove compatibility barrel when importer count reaches zero.
